query: South Korea KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell value weekly latest June 2026, recent trend in trillion won
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell flows as of July 7, 2026:

**Direction: Decisively NET SELLING at a record pace.**

- **H1 2026 (Jan–Jun 30):** Record **148.32 trillion won** (~$95–97bn) in foreign net selling — the largest first-half outflow ever.
- **YTD through July 3:** Cumulative net selling reached **157.3 trillion won** (~$102.5bn), meaning ~9 tn won more was added in just the first 3 trading days of H2.
- **Recent daily figure:** June 8 saw a single-session net sell of **1.24 trillion won**.
- **Concentration:** ~90% of selling was in Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix (Samsung foreign ownership at a 16-year low).

**Trend:** Not slowing. Brokerages expect continued H2 selling, driven by a ~6% won depreciation, profit-taking after the KOSPI's ~92–101% surge, and rotation into Japan. Notably, foreign ownership *share* still rose to 40.47% (from 36.65% in Jan) because chip-stock valuation gains outweighed outflows. The week of July 6–7 saw a sell-sidecar triggered and the index dropping ~5% intraday to ~7,650.

**Caveat:** A clean single-week early-July net figure isn't published in the sources; the strongest documented points are the cumulative H1 (148.3 tn) and YTD-through-July-3 (157.3 tn) figures, plus the June 8 daily figure.

sources.title: Foreign investors may have more Korean stocks to sell
sources.snippet: Foreign investors sold 157.3 trillion won ($102.5 billion) of shares on the KOSPI this year through July 3; index surged 91.9%.
